seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in Agno Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in Agno agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Agno, PII filtering failures for AI agents often occur due to the framework's lightweight, model-agnostic design that prioritizes speed over built-in data sanitization, leaving agents vulnerable to leaking sensitive information from multi-modal inputs and session memory. Agents processing images, audio, or structured outputs can inadvertently expose PII like emails, phone numbers, and addresses in responses without explicit filtering layers. This manifests particularly in multi-turn conversations where fabricated memory recall or unvalidated structured outputs bypass safety checks.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent extracts and returns full contact info (phone, email) from OCR without redaction
howItManifests.impact: Immediate PII exposure violating GDPR/CCPA compliance

howItManifests.scenario: Agentic session memory recalling customer support chats
howItManifests.symptom: Fabricated past interactions include real customer SSNs and addresses
howItManifests.impact: Compounding data leakage across conversation history

howItManifests.scenario: Structured output with nested contact schemas
howItManifests.symptom: Validation passes but raw PII fields (name, phone) appear in JSON response
howItManifests.impact: Downstream systems receive unfiltered sensitive data

howItManifests.symptom: One sub-agent extracts PII, others propagate it in coordination outputs
howItManifests.impact: Amplified exposure through agent collaboration patterns

detection.name: Regex PII Pattern Scanner
detection.description: Scans agent outputs for common PII patterns including emails, phones, SSNs using comprehensive regex
detection.code:
import re

def scan_pii_output(output: str) -> list:
    patterns = {
        'email': r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
        'phone': r'\b(?:\+?1[-.\s]?)?\(?([0-9]{3})\)?[-.\s]?([0-9]{3})[-.\s]?([0-9]{4})\b',
        'ssn': r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',
        'credit_card': r'\b(?:\d{4}[\s-]?){3}\d{4}\b'
    }
    findings = []
    for pii_type, pattern in patterns.items():
        matches = re.findall(pattern, output, re.IGNORECASE)
        if matches:
            findings.extend([{'type': pii_type, 'value': m} for m in matches])
    return findings

# Usage
result = scan_pii_output('Contact support@company.com or call 555-123-4567')
print(result)

fixes.name: Presidio PII Redactor Wrapper
fixes.description: Real-time PII redaction for all Agno agent outputs using Microsoft's Presidio
fixes.code:
pip install presidio-analyzer presidio-anonymizer

from presidio_analyzer import AnalyzerEngine
from presidio_anonymizer import AnonymizerEngine

def create_pii_filter():
    analyzer = AnalyzerEngine()
    anonymizer = AnonymizerEngine()
    
    def filter_pii(text: str) -> str:
        results = analyzer.analyze(text=text, language='en')
        return anonymizer.anonymize(text=text, analyzer_results=results).text
    
    return filter_pii

# Agno integration
pii_filter = create_pii_filter()

def safe_agent_output(agent_output: str) -> str:
    return pii_filter(agent_output)

# Usage
output = 'Contact john.doe@email.com or 555-123-4567'
print(safe_agent_output(output))  # 'Contact <EMAIL> or <PHONE_NUMBER>'

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade automation for PII filtering failures in Agno agents through its agent.verify() decorator that wraps agno.agent.run() calls. The platform automatically injects test cases covering multi-modal inputs, session memory scenarios, and structured outputs, detecting PII leakage with 99% precision across 50+ patterns. 

Remediation is automatic via Vex's code synthesis - when PII violations are detected, it generates and validates Presidio-based filters, memory sanitizers, and safe structured output schemas directly in your Agno codebase. The platform maintains zero false positives through ML-powered context analysis that understands Agno's specific memory and coordination patterns.

Integration takes 3 lines of code and provides continuous monitoring across all agent deployments, with automatic rollbacks for new PII leakage patterns.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install agno vex-sdk

from vex import verify
from agno import Agent

@verify(
    test_cases=[
        'Extract contact info from resume image',
        'Recall customer support conversation details',
        'Generate structured contact output',
        'Coordinate team agents on customer data'
    ],
    failure_threshold=0.1,
    remediation='auto_fix_pii'
)
def production_agno_agent(prompt: str):
    agent = Agent(name='production-agent')
    return agent.run(prompt)

# Vex auto-generates PII-safe version when failures detected
result = production_agno_agent('Process customer: John Doe, 555-123-4567')
print(result)
